mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lifts equity valuations by reducing the discount rate on future cash flows. When the policy rate is already near zero, each additional decline should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average during months with falling FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips and returns are persistently negative in easing months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above recent levels signals deteriorating household income and weaker demand, which should depress S&P 500 prices over the next month. The effect is strongest when payroll growth is also weak, confirming a broad labor-market slowdown rather than a measurement artifact.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses predictive power when PAYEMS is flat to rising during the same trigger window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating HOUST indicates stronger residential demand and improved cyclical growth expectations, which should lead S&P 500 returns higher over the next quarter as investors price firmer aggregate demand.
failure_condition: Reject if rising HOUST does not coincide with positive 60-day forward SPX returns, or if the average return in HOUST upturns is not above the unconditional mean over any 4-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO relative to its recent trend signals a broad industrial slowdown and weaker earnings momentum, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical sectors reprice quickly.
failure_condition: Reject if short-term industrial contractions are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the conditional mean return is not below zero for at least two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise, the signal identifies broad consumption growth supported by income gains, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect fails when retail sales improve without labor income support.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-PAYEMS upswings do not outperform single-series signals, or if positive 20-day SPX returns are not more frequent when both series are rising together over any 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS still anchored near zero implies an inflation surprise that raises real-rate concerns and compresses equity multiples. This should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation > 0.3% month over month while FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-surprise episodes with low policy rates do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal becomes positive in a majority of triggered cases across any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in PAYEMS indicates payroll losses and weaker earnings expectations, which should weigh on equity prices over the next quarter. The effect is strongest in periods when unemployment is also rising, confirming a genuine labor-market contraction.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll declines are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE pattern does not outperform PAYEMS alone in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A recovery in GDPC1 signals improving real final demand and supports higher earnings expectations, which should push S&P 500 returns higher over the next quarter. The signal is invalid if GDP growth is revised away or if output growth occurs without corresponding market gains.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth periods do not correspond to positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the relationship disappears after controlling for rolling revisions over any 2-year sample.
